options: Nodes will converge to the same data over time

options: Dynamically find network locations of other services

typescript.question: TypeScript is:
options: A completely different language from JS
A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to JS

typescript.question: The any type in TypeScript:
options: Opts out of type checking for that value

typescript.question: Union types (A | B) mean:
options: A value can be either type A or type B
A new type merging A and B

typescript.question: Generics in TypeScript allow:
options: Reusable code that works across multiple types with type safety

typescript.question: Type assertion (as Type) tells the compiler:
options: To throw an error if wrong
To treat a value as a specific type

typescript.question: Optional chaining (?.) in TypeScript:
options: Safely accesses nested properties that may be null/undefined
Is a TypeScript-only feature not in JS

typescript.question: Enums in TypeScript:
options: Define a set of named constants

typescript.question: The never type is used for:
options: Functions that never return (e.g. always throw or infinite loop)

typescript.question: Intersection types (A & B) mean:
options: A type that has all properties of both A and B

typescript.question: A type alias (type X = ...) differs from interface mainly in that:
options: Type aliases can represent primitives, unions, and tuples; interfaces are for object shapes

typescript.question: The keyof operator returns:
options: The values of an object type
A union of all keys of a type

typescript.question: Type narrowing in TypeScript refers to:
options: Refining a type to a more specific one inside a conditional block

typescript.question: Declaration files (.d.ts) are used to:
options: Provide type information for JavaScript libraries

typescript.question: strict mode in tsconfig enables:
options: A set of stricter type-checking rules for better safety

typescript.question: The infer keyword in TypeScript is used inside:
options: Conditional types to extract a type
